data.name: Beast Spells
data.description: Beginning at 18th level, you can cast many of your druid spells in any shape you assume using Wild Shape. You can perform the somatic and verbal components of a druid spell while in a beast shape, but you aren't able to provide material components.

consistency.class_match: Consistent. The feature is explicitly for the 'druid' class.
consistency.level_placement: Consistent. Level 18 is typically reserved for very powerful, capstone-like class abilities.

quality.clarity: High clarity. The mechanic is straightforward: removes common limitations of Wild Shape (Somatic/Verbal restrictions) while maintaining a key restriction (no Material components).
quality.power_level: High. Being able to cast spells in Wild Shape at high levels is a significant boost to the Druid's battlefield utility and survivability.

mechanics.core_function: Allows spellcasting while in Wild Shape forms.
mechanics.components_rule: Explicitly allows Somatic and Verbal components but forbids Material components. This interacts with the standard restriction of Wild Shape (the inability to use hands or manipulate objects for most forms), making this feature crucial for spellcasting viability in transformed states.
mechanics.interaction_notes: This feature directly addresses a common limitation where spellcasting components require specific physical actions (hands, mouth) often impossible in beast forms. The exclusion of Material components is a balanced restriction, preventing overly complex spellcasting while allowing essential utility/combat spells that lack material costs or only require verbal/somatic.
